ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Support for reading/writing simple feature ('sf') spatial objects from/to 'Parquet' files. 'Parquet' files are an open-source, column-oriented data storage format from Apache (<https://parquet.apache.org/>), now popular across programming languages. This implementation converts simple feature list geometries into well-known binary format for use by 'arrow', and coordinate reference system information is maintained in a standard metadata format.

Support for reading/writing simple feature ('sf') spatial objects from/to 'Parquet' files. 'Parquet' files are an open-source, column-oriented data storage format from Apache (<https://parquet.apache.org/>), now popular across programming languages. This implementation converts simple feature list geometries into well-known binary format for use by 'arrow', and coordinate reference system information is maintained in a standard metadata format.

name: r-sfarrow
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '0.4.1' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-sfarrow
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/sfarrow_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/sfarrow/sfarrow_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 0f5db73f8c3058b0aad6343d2376186028cc443a7df1c515b3a1171e14f454c3

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-arrow
    - r-dplyr
    - r-jsonlite
    - r-sf
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-arrow
    - r-dplyr
    - r-jsonlite
    - r-sf

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('sfarrow')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('sfarrow')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://github.com/wcjochem/sfarrow
  doc_url: https://wcjochem.github.io/sfarrow/
  license: MIT
  summary: Support for reading/writing simple feature ('sf') spatial objects from/to 'Parquet'
    files. 'Parquet' files are an open-source, column-oriented data storage format from
    Apache (<https://parquet.apache.org/>), now popular across programming languages.
    This implementation converts simple feature list geometries into well-known binary
    format for use by 'arrow', and coordinate reference system information is maintained
    in a standard metadata format.
  license_family: MIT
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/MIT'
    - LICENSE

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: sfarrow
# Title: Read/Write Simple Feature Objects ('sf') with 'Apache' 'Arrow'
# Version: 0.4.1
# Date: 2021-10-25
# Authors@R: person(given = "Chris", family = "Jochem", role = c("aut", "cre"), email = "w.c.jochem@soton.ac.uk", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-2192-5988"))
# Description: Support for reading/writing simple feature ('sf') spatial objects from/to 'Parquet' files. 'Parquet' files are an open-source, column-oriented data storage format from Apache (<https://parquet.apache.org/>), now popular across programming languages. This implementation converts simple feature list geometries into well-known binary format for use by 'arrow', and coordinate reference system information is maintained in a standard metadata format.
